summary: Large camera-system decompilation PR that replaced multiple `/// #...` stubs in `src/melee/cm/camera.c` with C implementations, expanded camera-related structs/types, and converted several raw `cm_803BCCA0._44[...]` accesses into named fields. The work covered gameplay camera update flow, camera input collection, pause-camera movement/zoom helpers, camera interpolation helpers, and camera mode initialization. Evidence includes added implementations for `Camera_8002A0C0`, `Camera_8002B3D4`, `Camera_8002B694`, `Camera_8002C010`, `Camera_8002E158`, and `Camera_8002FC7C`, plus header/signature/type updates. Review evidence is minimal; the only human comment in the slice concerns write access/merge permissions, not code review.
what_changed: Implemented several previously stubbed camera functions in `src/melee/cm/camera.c`, including `Camera_8002A0C0`, `Camera_8002B3D4`, `Camera_8002B694`, `Camera_8002C010`, `Camera_8002E158`, and `Camera_8002FC7C`.

Added helper inlines near the top of `camera.c`, including `vec_len(Vec3*)`, `abs_threshold_inline(f32 value, f32 threshold)`, `get_slot_pad(u8)`, `get_stick_x(HSD_PadStatus*)`, and `get_stick_y(HSD_PadStatus*)`.
Changed `Camera_80029AAC` from externally visible to `static`, indicating it is an internal camera helper.
Wrapped `Camera_80029BC4`/nearby code with `#pragma dont_inline on` and `#pragma dont_inline reset`, suggesting matching depended on controlling inlining behavior.
Changed `Camera_8002A28C` signature from `void Camera_8002A28C(void)` to `void Camera_8002A28C(CameraBounds* arg0)` and updated call sites, notably in the gameplay camera update path.

Reworked `Camera_8002B0E0` to use named `CameraUnkGlobals` fields such as `xD4`, `xD8`, `xDC`, `xE0`, and `xE4` instead of raw `_44[...]` indexing, while preserving single-player C-stick camera-depth behavior.

Implemented `Camera_8002B3D4` as an update gameplay camera routine that refreshes camera bounds, target FOV, camera transforms, stage smoothing, player/fighter z-threshold checks, camera distance tracking, and stage bounds averaging.

Implemented `Camera_8002B694` to populate `CameraInputs` from pad status for normal slots, a special disabled slot `5`, and aggregate slot `4` scanning all four controllers for stick/substick values above a `0.85f` threshold.
Converted several `cm_803BCCA0._44[...]` references to named struct fields such as `x9C`, `xA0`, `xAC`, `xB0`, `xB4`, `xB8`, `xBC`, and `xC0`.
Expanded camera headers/types: `types.h` received most of the non-code changes, while `camera.h` adjusted declarations and `forward.h` added a forward type declaration.
smart_moves: Moved `vec_len` earlier in the file so it could be reused by newly implemented camera code without relying on a later local helper definition.
Introduced small inline wrappers and an `abs_threshold_inline` helper to influence generated code shape while keeping repeated absolute-value threshold logic readable.
Used named fields in `CameraUnkGlobals`/`cm_803BCCA0` rather than continuing opaque `_44[index]` accesses, making later camera decomp work easier to search and refine.

Kept uncertainty explicit with comments such as `@todo figure out how to get cm_803BCCA0 to load into r3`, `@todo inline?`, `@remarks permuter jank`, and `@todo there is probably a bigger inline`, which preserves matching context for future cleanup instead of hiding fragile codegen constraints.
Used `#pragma dont_inline` around sensitive functions, showing that local inlining decisions were part of the matching strategy.
Structured `Camera_8002B3D4` around real camera concepts—bounds, transform, target FOV, target position/interest, smoothing, and player position checks—rather than leaving a monolithic stub.
decomp_lessons: For camera decomp, replacing raw parameter-table array indexing with provisional named fields is valuable even if names remain offset-style (`xAC`, `xB0`), because it documents repeated semantic use across zoom, bounds, pause camera, and C-stick camera-depth code.

Matching may require intentionally awkward temporaries, duplicate assignments, dummy conditions, or pointer aliases such as `CameraUnkGlobals* new_var`; preserve those when they are likely codegen scaffolding.
Small inline helpers can be introduced specifically to make threshold/ABS patterns match, but comments should identify where the inline is suspected rather than proven.

When decompiling a central update function like `Camera_8002B3D4`, implement the surrounding helper functions and type definitions in the same PR so signatures and struct field offsets can converge together.
Special controller slots can encode gameplay semantics: this PR shows slot `5` producing zeroed camera inputs and slot `4` aggregating across four controllers with threshold scans.
If exact register allocation or load ordering is unresolved, leave targeted TODOs rather than overclaiming a clean match rationale.
naming_conventions: Camera functions continue to use the project’s address-style names, e.g. `Camera_8002B3D4`, with comments adding semantic labels such as `update gameplay camera` when known.
Camera structs use PascalCase type names such as `CameraBounds`, `CameraTransformState`, `CameraInputs`, and `CameraUnkGlobals`.
Unknown struct members remain offset-style with `x` prefixes, e.g. `x2BC`, `x2C0`, `xA4`, `xAC`, `xE8`, which is consistent with decomp placeholder naming while still improving over anonymous arrays.
Global camera data follows existing symbol names such as `cm_80452C68`, `cm_803BCCA0`, `cm_803BCB64`, and static object pointer `cm_804D6464`.
Helper names are descriptive but local/simple, e.g. `vec_len`, `abs_threshold_inline`, `get_slot_pad`, `get_stick_x`, and `get_stick_y`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#pragma dont_inline on/reset` to prevent unwanted inlining around camera helper code.
Added inline helpers to control emitted ABS/threshold and pad-access patterns.

Introduced explicit temporaries such as `temp_f27`, `temp_f28`, `temp_f29_2`, `var_f30`, `var_f31`, `var_r0`, and pointer aliases like `new_var2` to influence register allocation and expression ordering.
Left matching-focused TODOs where codegen was not fully understood, including loading `cm_803BCCA0` into `r3` and suspected inline/permuter issues.
Used `sqrtf__Ff(total_dist)` in one distance calculation while `vec_len` uses `sqrtf`, indicating careful function-call selection for matching in different contexts.
Preserved seemingly redundant code in `Camera_8002B0E0`, including duplicate assignment `var_f2 = var_f1;` and an empty `if ((*new_var2).xD4 && (*new_var2).xD4) {}`, likely as codegen scaffolding.
review_feedback: No code review comments are present in the provided slice.
The only human text excerpt is from ribbanya telling `@sadkellz` they had been given write access and could merge after accepting the invite; this is process feedback, not technical review.
follow_up_queries: Inspect final merged `src/melee/cm/types.h` for the exact `CameraUnkGlobals`, `CameraBounds`, `CameraTransformState`, and `CameraInputs` field additions introduced by PR #1981.

Check whether later PRs renamed provisional `cm_803BCCA0` fields such as `x9C`, `xA0`, `xAC`, `xB0`, `xB4`, `xB8`, `xBC`, `xC0`, `xD4`, `xD8`, `xDC`, `xE0`, and `xE4` to semantic camera parameter names.
Search later camera PRs for cleanup of TODOs from this PR: `cm_803BCCA0 to load into r3`, `inline?`, `permuter jank`, and `there is probably a bigger inline`.
Compare `Camera_8002B3D4` and `Camera_8002B694` against later versions to see whether helper inlines or redundant codegen scaffolding were removed after matching improved.
